channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: McDonald's E. coli outbreak: Why only the Quarter Pounder was linked
item.description: McDonald's has voluntarily stopped selling its Quarter Pounder hamburger at restaurants across Colorado and other states after an E. coli outbreak was linked to the menu item.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Jarpe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: President Biden to apologize for 150-year Indian boarding school policy
item.description: President Joe Biden is preparing to issue an historic apology during his first diplomatic visit to a tribal nation as president on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Graham Lee Brewer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Georgia men who killed Ahmaud Arbery want a new trial
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — Three white men serving life in prison for murder after chasing and killing Ahmaud Arbery in 2020 returned to court Thursday to ask a judge for a new trial.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Ex-officer on trial in shooting death of Andre Hill thought his life was in danger, attorney says
item.description: An attorney for a white former Ohio police officer says the officer mistakenly shot and killed Andre Hill in 2020 because he saw a flash of light from keys in the Black man’s hand and thought he was about to be shot.
item.dc:creator: John Seewer, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: McDonald's customer files first lawsuit over E. Coli outbreak
item.description: A McDonald’s customer filed the first lawsuit against the fast food chain on Wednesday in connection with an E. coli outbreak linked to the chain’s Quarter Pounder. Eric Stelly, who filed the suit in Cook County Circuit Court in Illinois, ate food from McDonald's Greeley, Colorado, location on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Los Angeles DA to announce decision on Menendez brothers case Thursday
item.description: Los Angeles County District Attorney George Gascón has scheduled a news conference for Thursday afternoon to announce whether his office will recommend Erik and Lyle Menendez be resentenced in light of new information in their murder case.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Kiszla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors are set to reveal whether they favor resentencing Erik and Lyle Menendez
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Prosecutors will announce Thursday whether or not they recommend resentencing Erik and Lyle Menendez for the 1989 killings of their parents in Beverly Hills. The brothers were sentenced in 1996 to life in prison without the possibility of parole.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Delphi murders: Misfiled report from 2017 put Allen in investigators' sights in 2022
item.description: DELPHI, Ind. – As Day 6 of testimony in the Delphi murders trials began, the state began to lay the groundwork for its case against Richard Allen. Allen is charged with four counts of murder in connection with the February 2017 deaths of Abby Williams and Libby German near the Monon High Bridge.
item.dc:creator: Max Lewis, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia officials say they thwarted an attempt to crash a state election website
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Georgia election officials acted quickly earlier this month to thwart an attempt to flood the state's absentee voter portal in an apparent attempt to crash the site, the secretary of state's office said.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Beyoncé expected to join Harris at Houston rally: Reports
item.description: Beyoncé is poised to get in "Formation" behind Vice President Kamala Harris' White House bid. The music superstar will reportedly be joining the vice president at a Friday campaign rally in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Beyonce expected to join Harris at Houston rally
item.description: Beyoncé is expected to appear Friday in her hometown of Houston at a rally for Vice President Kamala Harris, according to three people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard will seek to arrest political opponents as she faces eviction
item.description: Dolton Mayor Tiffany Henyard has suggested she'll seek to arrest political opponents, many of whom have raised concerns about her lavish spending on the taxpayers dime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Students, families report overcrowded school buses in Utah
item.description: Families are raising concerns after students have reported overcrowded school buses in the Alpine School District.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Tracy, NewsNation

item.title: Georgia's Gullah-Geechee community seeks path forward after deadly dock collapse
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — The partial collapse of a ferry dock that claimed the lives of seven people attending a cultural festival has left a historic Black community in Georgia struggling with how to move forward.
item.dc:creator: TERRY TANG and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The 3 men convicted of murdering Ahmaud Arbery motion for new trial
item.description: Travis and Gregory McMichael armed themselves with guns and jumped in a pickup truck to chase Arbery, a 25-year-old Black man, after they saw him run past their house on Feb. 23, 2020.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World on pace for significantly more warming without immediate climate action, report warns
item.description: The world is on a path to get 1.8 degrees Celsius (3.2 Fahrenheit) warmer than it is now, a United Nations report said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: DNA links suspected serial killer to 19-year-old's cold case murder in Illinois
item.description: Authorities have linked a suspected serial killer to the kidnapping and murder of a 19-year-old Illinois woman, more than 45 years after her death.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Homemade meal sends dozens of Maryland employees to the hospital
item.description: NAFCO Wholesale Seafood Distributors reported that 46 of its workers at its Jessup, Maryland, facility were taken to nearby hospitals after being sickened by homemade food.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgia voter website hit with cyberattack, likely from foreign source: official
item.description: An official with the Georgia secretary of state’s office said it stopped a cyberattack last week that targeted its absentee ballot website.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ron Ely, TV's 'Tarzan' in the 1960s, dies at 86
item.description: Ron Ely, the tall, musclebound actor who played the title character in the 1960s NBC series “Tarzan,” has died at age 86.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Before Taylor Swift show in New Orleans, a homeless encampment is forced to move
item.description: In anticipation of three Taylor Swift concerts, which could draw around 150,000 visitors to New Orleans' Superdome this weekend, state authorities on Wednesday began clearing a nearby homeless encampment.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN McGILL, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Florida mailman stabs lawncare worker over parking: deputies
item.description: The Hillsborough County Sheriff's Office has released more information about the events that may have led up to a deadly stabbing Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KRON4

item.title: People 50 and older should get pneumococcal vaccine, U.S. health officials recommend
item.description: U.S. health officials on Wednesday recommended that people 50 and older get a shot against bacteria that can cause pneumonia and other dangerous illnesses.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: TV host Adam Richman says production crew robbed while filming in North London
item.description: TV host Adam Richman, best known for starring in "Man v. Food," says he and his production crew were robbed while filming a show in the UK.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, Fox 5

item.title: Quarter Pounders are off the menu at 20% of McDonald's in US as E. coli cases are investigated
item.description: McDonald’s worked Wednesday to reassure customers that its U.S. restaurants are safe as federal investigators tried to pinpoint the cause of a deadly E. coli outbreak linked to the fast-food giant's Quarter Pounder hamburgers. McDonald's pulled Quarter Pounders from one-fifth of its U.S.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Pennsylvania high court gives voters provisional option if their mail ballots get rejected
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — Pennsylvania's highest court on Wednesday said people whose mail ballots are rejected for not following technical procedures in state law can cast provisional ballots, a decision sure to affect some of the thousands of mail-in votes likely to be rejected this fall.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: North Carolina government calculates Hurricane Helene damages, needs at least $53B
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — The catastrophic flooding and destruction caused by Hurricane Helene in western North Carolina likely caused at least a record $53 billion in damages and recovery needs, Gov. Roy Cooper's administration said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Quarter of young Black men say they will vote for Trump: Survey
item.description: Despite one-fourth of young Black men saying they would cast their ballot for Trump, they repeatedly identify Harris as the strongest candidate on the issues.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bad state data may misdirect nearly $1 billion in federal funds to replace lead pipes
item.description: ST. LOUIS (AP) — The Environmental Protection Agency is at risk of misallocating nearly $1 billion in lead pipe replacement funding to the wrong states because it didn’t verify inaccurate data provided by Texas and Florida, an agency watchdog announced.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Former 'American Idol' hopeful arrested on child porn charges
item.description: Police say they found hundreds of images and videos of child pornography on a smartphone while searching his home.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Richardson/ KFOR, Fox 5

item.title: Fallout from 2023 Hamas attack lingers as Jews worldwide prepare for a holiday meant for joy
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Customarily, Simchat Torah is one of the most joyous days of the Jewish calendar, highlighted by exuberant dancing around a Torah. Jewish leaders say the joy will return during this week's celebrations, even as Jews recall that last year's Simchat Torah overlapped with Oct.
item.dc:creator: DAVID CRARY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump selling 'MAGADonald's' shirts after drive-thru appearance
item.description: After Trump’s visit to the Bucks County franchise, McDonald’s sought to emphasize its political neutrality and said it does not endorse political candidates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Mike Jeffries, former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO, arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Michael Jeffries and two other men have been arrested on sex trafficking and interstate prostitution charges, a spokesperson for federal prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump makes more debunked claims about FEMA as he visits storm damaged North Carolina
item.description: Trump was asked whether it was helpful to criticize hurricane relief workers after the Federal Emergency Management Agency recently paused work in the area because of reports they could be targeted by militia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Helicopter crashes into radio tower in Houston, killing 4
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Four people aboard a helicopter were killed when it crashed into a radio tower in Houston and burst into flames, officials said. The helicopter went down just before 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pinellas County man recovering after near-death experience with flesh-eating bacteria
item.description: A Pinellas County man is sharing his near death experience after contracting Vibrio Vulnificus, a flesh-eating bacteria. This comes as the Florida Department of Health is warning people to avoid floodwaters, as cases of the infection continue to rise after back-to-back hurricanes.
item.dc:creator: Melissa Marino, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tropical Storm Oscar brings heavy rainfall and flash flooding to eastern Cuba
item.description: Tropical Storm Oscar is producing heavy rainfall and flash flooding over eastern Cuba, The National Hurricane Center said.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC
